sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple rectangle in the game area changed to white! When I clicked the white box in the UI, the purple rectangle became white. This means:
1. The UI boxes are "color selectors"
2. Clicking a color selector changes the selected object to that color
3. The white line under a box indicates which color is currently "selected"
Now I need to figure out what the goal is. Looking at the top-left area:
- White rectangle on top of purple rectangle
This might be the "target configuration" - white on top, purple on bottom.
Currently in the game area:
- White rectangle (was purple, changed after clicking white)
- White rectangle below it
So now I have white on top of white. I need to make the bottom one purple to match the target. Let me click the purple box in the UI and then click the bottom white rectangle.
Wait, let me think about this more carefully. The selection indicator (white line) moved to the white box. So I think:
1. Clicking a UI color box selects that color
2. Then clicking an object changes it to that color
Or maybe:
1. Clicking an object selects it
2. Clicking a UI color box changes the selected object to that color
Let me try clicking the bottom white rectangle first, then the purple UI box.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple rectangle changed to white when I clicked the white UI box!
# Let me check the current state of the game objects
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Both rectangles are now white (id=12 and id=13). The top one changed from purple to white when I clicked the white UI box. Now I need to change the bottom one to purple to match the target configuration (white on top, purple on bottom).
Let me click on the bottom white rectangle first to select it, then click the purple UI box.
